element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Immediate; player gains confidence before enemies appear
Introduced early as primary tutorial threat; low risk
Progressive gap widths teach spacing and timing
Found in blocks; teaches block-breaking and power mechanics
Introduces ranged offense; encourages exploration of alternate paths
More complex than Goomba; can be used as platform or weapon
Introduces vertically-oriented level design and darkness theming
Optional; rewards exploration and hidden block discovery
Endgame threat; combines all learned mechanics under pressure
Capstone encounter; single repeated pattern learnable through trial
Temporary power-up; teaches anticipation and aggression windows
Requires timing and jump precision; cannot be stomped normally
Fundamentally different from stomp/jump; teaches pulling and throwing
Requires picking/throwing strategy; cannot be avoided via stomp
Transforms level navigation; enables sequence breaks
Mini-boss gauntlets; combines multiple mechanics simultaneously

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Slow introduction of core mechanics in 1-1/1-2, gradual accumulation of threat types per world, cyclical escalation per game (SMB1→Lost Levels→SMB2→SMB3 increase overall difficulty), boss encounters punctuate each world conclusion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Blocks containing hidden items/power-ups scattered above expected path; high blocks signal optional vertical exploration; background patterns hint at world thematic transitions
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Warp zones hidden in clouds and pipes; unused space above visible playfield rewards jumping exploration; alternate power-up pathways marked by breakable blocks

difficulty_curve.early_game: SMB1 Worlds 1-3: linear introduction of core platforming and enemy types; Goombas and basic jumps dominate; mushroom power-ups frequent
difficulty_curve.mid_game: SMB1 Worlds 4-8 + Lost Levels: enemy density increases, Piranha Plants and Bullet Bills add spatial complexity, fire-heavy sections demand ranged offense or avoidance, Lost Levels introduces poison mushrooms and harder jump sequences
difficulty_curve.late_game: SMB2 + SMB3: SMB2 fundamentally alters mechanics (throwing vs stomping), SMB3 reintroduces stomp mechanics with flight/tail power-ups but adds Fortress levels and airship sequences with combined threats
notable_spikes: Lost Levels World A+ (optional harder worlds, poison mushrooms, tighter timings)
SMB3 World 7-8 (Fortress and Airship levels combine multiple mechanics)
SMB3 final boss Bowser phase 2 (airship section with projectiles)

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe introduction with walking, jumping over gaps, first enemy (Goomba), first power-up (mushroom), underground cave variant. Establishes core loop without punishment.
Introduces 1-2 new enemy types per world, combines earlier mechanics with new threats (Koopa Troopas, Piranha Plants), maintains playability while raising stakes.
End-of-world level that combines all threats encountered in that world; Bowser or mini-boss encounter; darker/red theming signals danger zone.
Darker palette, vertical emphasis, cave-like theming. Often second or third level per world. Changes visual/spatial feel without introducing entirely new mechanics.
Pulling/throwing vegetables replaces stomp mechanic; Mask/Beezos cannot be jumped on; climbing/vines emphasized. Functions as complete genre reframing mid-compilation.
Each world emphasizes different power-up (Raccoon Tail enables flying, Frog Suit enables swimming). Level design expects players to obtain and use specific power-up for progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: SMB3 World 3 (water world with Frog Suit)
